auto_approve.mechanism: Users can configure permanent permissions through permissions.json files so that specific MCP tools do not require per-invocation approval; auto-run functionality can also be enabled globally.

enterprise_management.mechanism: Cursor does not document organization-level managed MCP policy controls beyond the general hook and rule enterprise scopes.

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Server configuration supports variable interpolation using ${env:NAME} for environment variables, ${userHome} for the user's home directory, and ${workspaceFolder} for the workspace root — not limited to env blocks alone.

marketplace.mechanism: Cursor ships an in-IDE MCP Marketplace for discovering and installing community MCP servers with one-click installation.

oauth_support.mechanism: Remote servers using the streamable-http transport support OAuth authentication; static client credentials can be used instead of dynamic registration. The documentation explicitly notes OAuth as a streamable-http capability.

resource_referencing.mechanism: MCP resources (structured data sources) are documented as a supported protocol capability alongside tools, prompts, roots, elicitation, and Apps.

tool_filtering.mechanism: Cursor does not document per-server tool allowlists or blocklists for MCP servers.

transport_types.mechanism: Cursor documents three MCP transports: stdio for local processes managed by Cursor, sse for Server-Sent Events (local or remote, multi-user), and streamable-http for HTTP-based servers (local or remote, multi-user) with OAuth authentication support.

providerExtensions.name: Three MCP transport mechanisms
providerExtensions.summary: Supports stdio (local, single-user), sse (local/remote, multi-user via Server-Sent Events), and streamable-http (local/remote, multi-user via HTTP) transports chosen by the server configuration.

providerExtensions.name: Project and global MCP config files
providerExtensions.summary: Project-scope servers live in .cursor/mcp.json at the repo root; user-global servers live in ~/.cursor/mcp.json.

providerExtensions.name: Environment variables in server env blocks
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server env maps pass environment variables (including secrets sourced from the user's shell) to stdio servers; configuration also supports ${env:NAME}, ${userHome}, and ${workspaceFolder} interpolation syntax.

providerExtensions.name: In-IDE MCP Marketplace
providerExtensions.summary: Cursor exposes an MCP Marketplace inside the IDE for browsing and one-click installing community MCP servers without editing mcp.json by hand.

providerExtensions.name: permissions.json for permanent MCP tool approval
providerExtensions.summary: Users can configure permanent per-tool approval through permissions.json files, preventing repeated approval prompts for trusted MCP tools.

providerExtensions.name: Full MCP protocol capability set: tools, prompts, resources, roots, elicitation, Apps
providerExtensions.summary: Cursor supports the full MCP protocol capability surface including tools (executable functions), prompts (templated workflows), resources (structured data sources), roots (server-initiated URI inquiries), elicitation (user information requests), and Apps (interactive UI extensions).
